writes: localStorage['docvault_token']    — line 63: initial access token from POST /api/auth/login
localStorage['docvault_refresh_token'] — line 64: refresh token from POST /api/auth/login

calls.notes: Validates against hardcoded admin@docvault.local / bcrypt hash (no database). Returns {token: 'eyJ...', refreshToken: 'eyJ...'} on 200; {error: 'Email and password required'} on 400; {error: 'Invalid credentials'} on 401.

BUG (FR-007): Backend verifies the JWT correctly with jwt.verify() but returns {session:{userId, email, createdAt}} — shape copied from the legacy /session/login handler — instead of {token, refreshToken}. AuthContext then executes refreshResponse.data.token.split('.') at line 75; token is undefined, crashing with TypeError unconditionally on every successful credential submission. The localStorage.setItem at AuthContext.jsx:85 and the isAuthenticated=true transition are never reached.
